entities.name: Internet of Careers
entities.context: A collective high-fidelity data exchange model where individual qualitative reflections serve as shared capital to resolve hiring conflicts of interest

entities.name: Market for Lemons
entities.context: Economic theory about information asymmetry where both parties present curated, low-fidelity versions of truth

entities.name: Network effect
entities.context: Principle where the utility of the collective increases exponentially with the honesty and depth of individual contributions

entities.name: Information asymmetry
entities.context: The fundamental problem in hiring where candidates hide flaws and employers hide culture issues

entities.name: Honest reflection
entities.context: The key behavior the platform must incentivize over performative profile polishing

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company, implied context for this hiring/career data exchange framework

relationships.context: The Internet of Careers model aims to solve the Market for Lemons problem in hiring
The model relies on network effects where collective utility grows with honest participation
The framework addresses information asymmetry by creating incentives for high-fidelity data sharing
The platform must incentivize honest reflection to succeed; without it, the system collapses into a standard job board
This conceptual framework appears to underpin Careerspan's approach to career data exchange
